what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record that declares an attachment of type board_items linking the dragnet-development channel to the board item item-fe58f9a5 and records when it was added.
observations: The entire content is YAML frontmatter with no body text
Fields are id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added
target uses a board:// URI pointing to item-fe58f9a5
standouts: The target is a board:// URI, making this a typed cross-system pointer
It is purely metadata: a timestamped attachment reference with no payload
conventions: Uses a board:// URI scheme to reference a board item
Uses an ISO 8601 UTC timestamp for the added field
Follows a flat key/value attachment record layout
concerns: No explanatory payload or reason for the attachment is provided
The referenced board item content is not included, so the file is not self-contained
No author or provenance beyond the timestamp is recorded
notes: A timestamped channel-attachment pointer of type board_items that references board item fe58f9a5 in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What does board item item-fe58f9a5 represent?
What does the board_items attachment_type signify semantically?
Was this attachment created automatically or manually?
Is there a payload or content expected to accompany this metadata?
intention_signals: It appears to record that a specific board item is attached to the dragnet-development channel memory, creating a discoverable, timestamped association between a channel and a board item.
novelty: Use of a board:// URI scheme to target a board item inside a channel attachment metadata record.
contribution: Provides a lightweight, timestamped, typed link between a channel memory attachment and a specific board item, preserving the fact and time of the association.
summary_for_embedding: Channel attachment metadata record att-809262b8 of type board_items linking the dragnet-development channel to board item item-fe58f9a5, added on 2026-06-24.
intention_for_embedding: To record a typed, timestamped reference from channel memory to a specific board item.
